    • Stepwise Gaussian graphical model selection
    • Regulation networks from Dynalytics event data
    • Information-filtering networks with TMFG and LoGo
    • Ising networks for binary data
    • Mixed graphical models
    • Edge-weight stability and split-half reliability
    • Relative-importance networks
    • Visualizing bootstrap, centrality and difference results (base R)
    • Visualizing networks with cograph

Changelog

Source: NEWS.md

psychnets 0.4.2

  • First CRAN release.

  • mgm_fit(..., moderators = ) (the moderated mixed graphical model, read with condition()) now runs on psychnets’ own base-R penalized-IRLS kernel and is KKT-certified. It previously required the compiled glmnet package and silently ignored native =. Every estimator in the package is now pure base R by default; glmnet and glasso remain optional reference engines selected with native = FALSE.

  • The base moderated kernel covers gaussian and binary nodes (the documented mgm_fit() scope) and errors explicitly on a categorical node with more than two levels, which needs native = FALSE.

Developed by Mohammed Saqr, Sonsoles López-Pernas.

Site built with pkgdown 2.2.1.
